name: Transistor (Bipolar and Field-Effect)
tagline: Solid-state charge control in semiconductors — amplification and logic without heated cathodes
problem:
Vacuum tubes gave the world amplification, but they also gave it heat, fragility, and maintenance marathons — a computer should not consume more power than its city block. The transistor promises the same controlled current modulation with a cold junction inside a crystal: no heated cathode, no glass envelope, paths toward micrometre scale if surfaces can be passivated and junction depths controlled.

Bipolar devices inject carriers across a thin base; field-effect devices steer charge with an electric field through an oxide — each family trades speed, voltage tolerance, and fabrication complexity. The obstacle is cleanliness at atomic levels: a stray sodium ion in oxide shifts threshold voltage; a pinhole in passivation lets humidity drift parameters.

Lithography scaled junctions from millimetres to nanometres, but the transistor’s cultural lesson predates fabs: small reliable switches compose universality — logic is geology built from sand if you can pattern dopants honestly.

For recovery, protect not only crystal pullers but also the tacit craft of diffusion furnaces, implanter tuning, and wire-bond ergonomics.
overview:
Planar silicon processing grows thermal oxide, patterns windows in photoresist, introduces dopants by diffusion or implant, metallises contacts, and passivates surfaces so junctions stay stable in humid air. CMOS pairs n-channel and p-channel MOSFETs so static power stays tiny except during switching — enabling chips with billions of gates.

Packaging bonds aluminium or copper wires from die pads to lead frames, moulds epoxy, and trims legs before burn-in screens infant failures. Power discretes parallel thousands of cells; IGBTs merge MOS gates with bipolar conductivity for traction inverters.

Characterisation — curve tracers, S-parameters, reliability ovens — translates physics into datasheets designers trust. Electromigration and hot-carrier wear appear as accelerated ageing curves.

Discrete transistors remain teaching instruments: you can still see a TO-220 tab and imagine heat leaving the junction.

principles.name: PN junction physics
principles.explanation: Diffusion and drift balance set built-in potential; bias modulates depletion width and injection currents exponentially with voltage in ideal diode law.

principles.name: Field effect in MOS
principles.explanation: Vertical field inverts surface from p to n (or inverse), forming conductive channel whose sheet resistance gate voltage modulates.

principles.name: Carrier mobility
principles.explanation: Electrons move faster than holes in silicon — nMOS tends faster in equal geometry; strain silicon boosts mobility in advanced nodes.

principles.name: Scaling constant-field
principles.explanation: Shrink dimensions while adjusting doping and oxide thickness to preserve internal fields — until quantum tunnelling ends Dennard scaling.

components.name: Emitter, base, collector regions
components.position: Implanted or diffused mesas then planar
components.madeFrom: Silicon with arsenic, phosphorus, boron profiles

components.name: Gate oxide and polysilicon gate
components.madeFrom: SiO₂ historically; high-k HfO₂ in advanced nodes

components.name: Silicide and metal contacts
components.madeFrom: TiSi₂ or CoSi₂ then copper dual damascene in fabs

components.name: Package and heatsink
components.madeFrom: TO-220 epoxy, Cu base plates in power modules

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Transistor (Bipolar and Field-Effect) is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

rawMaterials.name: Silicon wafers
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Czochralski silicon wafers from established suppliers; early off-world electronics imports packaged discretes until local crystal pullers and clean chemistry justify wafer fabs

rawMaterials.name: Dopant species
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Arsine, phosphine, and diborane diluted for implant sources — exhaust abatement is non-negotiable

rawMaterials.name: Photoresists and acids
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Electronic-grade photoresists, developers, and HF-containing wet benches — neutralise wastes deliberately, not down the drain

buildSteps.title: Start from wafers you can measure, not mystery bags
buildSteps.description: Map resistivity and crystal orientation; clean with validated RCA or piranha sequences appropriate to your safety kit. Always know oxide thickness targets before dopant steps — they couple.
buildSteps.warningNote: Skip cleaning once and weeks of work become archaeology

buildSteps.title: Pattern a test die of simple structures
buildSteps.description: Include Van der Pauw sheets, ring oscillators, and long serpentines to catch shorts. Align photomasks under microscope before exposing whole wafers — misalignment shows as uniform leakage, maddeningly symmetric.

buildSteps.title: Implant or diffuse, then anneal with documented ramps
buildSteps.description: Record peak temperature and soak time on charts tied to wafer IDs. Measure junction depth indirectly via sheet resistance and CV if you lack SIMS.
buildSteps.warningNote: Channeling skews profiles — tilt wafers per recipe

buildSteps.title: Metallise, bond, and package without smothering heat
buildSteps.description: Choose wire gauge and loop height for inductance limits; attach heatsinks before power tests. Pull-test bonds on sacrificial units.
buildSteps.warningNote: Epoxy outgassing corrodes bonds in hermetic failures

buildSteps.title: Screen parts and publish honest limits
buildSteps.description: Burn-in at modest overstress catches early deaths; curve-trace safe operating area including temperature derating. A datasheet lie becomes a field fire — mark secondary breakdown regions clearly.
buildSteps.warningNote: Never ship without thermal resistance numbers

history.event: Point-contact transistor demonstrated at Bell Labs.
Bipolar junction transistor theory by Shockley.
MOSFET commercialisation begins as planar oxide quality improves.

inventors.name: William Shockley
inventors.contribution: Junction transistor and semiconductor theory leadership

impact:
Transistors shrank radios to pockets and later put billions of switches on one die — the hinge between industrial electricity and planetary computation.

Understanding them is prerequisite to reading any modern datasheet.

videos.title: MOSFET operation
videos.description: Solid-state electronics courses on threshold, saturation, and body effect.

sources.title: Nature (1953) — semiconductor junction devices (DOI)
sources.note: Peer-reviewed primary literature anchor for solid-state amplification history

sources.title: IEEE Standards Association
sources.note: Solid-state devices and integrated circuit standards
